title: Greatly Flaky Brazilian Masarepa Flatbread
description: This lacto-vegetarian friendly flatbread is a peppery and flaky Brazilian dish made with white masarepa and double rise bread, perfect for adventurous eaters.
instructions: In a large bowl, combine the white masarepa, all purpose flour, yeast, salt, and sugar.
Gradually add the warm water to the bowl and mix until a dough forms.
Knead the dough for 10 minutes until it becomes smooth and elastic.
Place the dough in a greased bowl, cover it with a damp cloth, and let it rise in a warm place for 1 hour.
Punch down the dough and divide it into 4 equal pieces.
Roll out each piece into a thin circle.
Brush the tops with vegetable oil and sprinkle with black pepper.
Bake the flatbreads for 20 minutes or until they are golden brown and crispy.
Let the flatbreads cool for 5 minutes before serving.
